ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 206, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 3 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,290.00. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO.3 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 15 DAYS NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFFS MUST PROVIDE THE CURRENT RESIDENTIAL ADDRESS AND TELEPHONE NUMBERS FOR EACH WITNESS IDENTIFIED IN ITS RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 3. IF AFTER A 'DILIGENT SEARCH AND REASONABLE INQUIRY,' PLAINTIFFS ARE UNABLE TO PROVIDE RESPONSIVE ANSWERS, THEY SHALL 'SET FORTH THE EFFORTS MADE TO SECURE THE INFORMATION' AND STATE THAT THEY HAVE NO RESPONSIVE INFORMATION. CCP 2030.220; DEYO V. KILBOURNE (1978) 84 CAL.APP.3D 771, 782. PLAINTIFFS' ATTORNEY OF RECORD, THE LAW OFFICE OF KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP SHALL PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,100.00 TO COUNSEL FOR UNION CARBIDE: MCKENNA, LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP WITHIN 15 DAYS. SANCTIONS ARE WARRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS' POSITION LACKED SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
